def _stats_job_entry(
    *,
    applied: bool,
    visa_sponsorship,
    fetched: str = "",
    last_seen: str = "",
) -> dict:
    return {
        "applied": applied,
        "visa_sponsorship": visa_sponsorship,
        "fetched": fetched,
        "last_seen": last_seen,
    }


def _partition_stored_jobs_for_stats(
    stored_jobs: list[dict],
    *,
    user_id: int | None,
    job_tracking: dict,
    alias_index: dict[tuple[str, str, str], dict],
    company_name: str,
    company: dict,
    country_key: str,
    visa_only: bool,
    position_filters: PositionFilters,
) -> tuple[list[dict], list[dict]]:
    jobs: list[dict] = []
    rejected_jobs: list[dict] = []
    expected_locations = company_expected_locations(company, catalog_country=country_key)

    for job in stored_jobs:
        fails_gate, _ = _job_fails_office_location_gate_cached(job, expected_locations)
        track: dict = {}
        if user_id:
            track = resolve_track_flags(
                job_tracking,
                alias_index,
                country=country_key,
                company_name=company_name,
                job=job,
            )
            wrong_location = effective_wrong_location(fails_gate=fails_gate, track=track)
            view = position_view_from_row(track, wrong_location=wrong_location)
            if view.bucket == PositionBucket.NOT_FOR_ME:
                continue
        elif catalog_not_for_me(job) or fails_gate:
            continue

        if visa_only and job.get("visa_sponsorship") is not True:
            continue

        applied = bool(track.get("applied")) if user_id else bool(job.get("applied"))
        rejected = as_bool(track.get("rejected")) if user_id else as_bool(job.get("rejected"))
        flags = TrackingFlags(
            applied=applied,
            rejected=rejected,
            not_for_me=bool(track.get("not_for_me")) if user_id else bool(job.get("not_for_me")),
            looking_to_apply=bool(track.get("looking_to_apply")) if user_id else False,
        )
        if derive_bucket(flags) == PositionBucket.REJECTED:
            rejected_jobs.append(_stats_job_entry(
                applied=applied,
                visa_sponsorship=job.get("visa_sponsorship"),
            ))
            continue
        if not passes_position_filters(flags, position_filters):
            continue
        jobs.append(_stats_job_entry(
            applied=applied,
            visa_sponsorship=job.get("visa_sponsorship"),
            fetched=job.get("fetched", ""),
            last_seen=job.get("last_seen", ""),
        ))

    return jobs, rejected_jobs


def _append_tracked_orphans_for_stats(
    jobs: list[dict],
    rejected_jobs: list[dict],
    *,
    country_key: str,
    company_name: str,
    job_tracking: dict,
    visa_only: bool,
    position_filters: PositionFilters,
) -> None:
    listed_urls = {normalize_job_url(j.get("url", "")) for j in jobs}
    listed_urls.update(normalize_job_url(j.get("url", "")) for j in rejected_jobs)
    listed_keys = {job_idempotency_key(j.get("url", "")) for j in jobs if j.get("url")}
    listed_keys.update(
        job_idempotency_key(j.get("url", "")) for j in rejected_jobs if j.get("url")
    )
    listed_keys.discard("")
    for (t_country, t_company, t_url), track in job_tracking.items():
        ctx = _OrphanTrackContext(
            country_key=country_key,
            company_name=company_name,
            t_country=t_country,
            t_company=t_company,
            t_url=t_url,
            track=track,
            listed_urls=listed_urls,
            listed_keys=listed_keys,
        )
        if any_of(ctx, _ORPHAN_TRACK_SKIP_RULES):
            continue
        if visa_only and track.get("visa_sponsorship") is not True:
            continue
        applied = bool(track.get("applied"))
        rejected = as_bool(track.get("rejected"))
        flags = TrackingFlags(
            applied=applied,
            rejected=rejected,
            not_for_me=bool(track.get("not_for_me")),
            looking_to_apply=bool(track.get("looking_to_apply")),
        )
        if rejected:
            rejected_jobs.append(_stats_job_entry(applied=applied, visa_sponsorship=None))
            continue
        if not passes_position_filters(flags, position_filters):
            continue
        jobs.append(_stats_job_entry(applied=applied, visa_sponsorship=None))


def summarize_company_for_stats(
    company: dict,
    *,
    country_key: str,
    filters: FlattenFilters,
    ctx: PanelContext,
    alias_index: dict[tuple[str, str, str], dict],
) -> dict | None:
    company_name = company.get("name", "")
    if filters.country_key and filters.country_key != "all":
        if not company_visible_for_country_filter(
            company, filters.country_key, catalog_country=country_key,
        ):
            return None

    stored_jobs = company.get("matching_jobs") or []
    header = _company_header_state(
        user_id=ctx.user_id,
        country_key=country_key,
        company_name=company_name,
        company=company,
        stored_jobs=stored_jobs,
        job_tracking=ctx.job_tracking,
        company_tracking=ctx.company_tracking,
    )
    if _skip_company_before_jobs(
        company,
        filters=filters,
        country_key=country_key,
        country_filter=filters.country_key,
        location_filter=filters.location_filter,
        header=header,
    ):
        return None

    jobs, rejected = _partition_stored_jobs_for_stats(
        stored_jobs,
        user_id=ctx.user_id,
        job_tracking=ctx.job_tracking,
        alias_index=alias_index,
        company_name=company_name,
        company=company,
        country_key=country_key,
        visa_only=filters.visa_only,
        position_filters=filters.position_filters,
    )
    if ctx.user_id:
        _append_tracked_orphans_for_stats(
            jobs,
            rejected,
            country_key=country_key,
            company_name=company_name,
            job_tracking=ctx.job_tracking,
            visa_only=filters.visa_only,
            position_filters=filters.position_filters,
        )
    if _skip_company_after_jobs(
        filters=filters,
        jobs=jobs,
        not_for_me_jobs=[],
        rejected_jobs=rejected,
        header=header,
    ):
        return None

    sort_ts = company_newest_job_fetched(jobs, company)
    return {
        "country": country_key,
        "jobs": jobs,
        "positions_rejected": len(rejected),
        "company_applied": header["company_applied"],
        "positions_applied_all": header["positions_applied_all"],
        "newest_job_fetched": sort_ts,
        "latest_fetched": sort_ts,
    }

content.text:
Investigation found several real problems with admin panel data — not just display bugs, but how the numbers were computed.

## Root causes

1. **Panel stats used the full board flatten path** — `GET /api/admin/panel-stats` called `flatten_companies()` over the entire catalog, loading every job (including ~3.7MB of descriptions for Germany alone) and running expensive per-job location/tracking logic. On your EC2 data that takes **~2 minutes for Germany** and **~2+ minutes for all countries**, so the section was often wrong, empty, or stale.

2. **Misleading numbers side-by-side** — Overview shows raw catalog counts (578 stored roles) while panel stats show actionable open roles (~197 after applied/rejected/hidden/wrong-location). Both were technically correct but looked broken without explanation.

3. **Stale “New jobs” in Last fetch meta** — That column used `country_meta.last_fetch_new_jobs`, which docs say is legacy catalog metadata only and must not be used for UI stats.

4. **Tracking totals used `SUM(applied)`** — Should count rows, not sum flags (same today, but fragile).

5. **Config omitted Redis/countries store** — Backend returned it; the UI didn’t show it.

## Fixes applied

| Area | Change |
|------|--------|
| **Stats engine** | New `flatten_companies_for_stats()` — bulk DB load, no descriptions, no history/MCP, fast tracking index, lightweight job classification |
| **Board perf** | Cache `company_expected_locations` once per company in `partition_stored_jobs` (helps all panel reads) |
| **Admin API** | `/api/admin/panel-stats` now uses the stats path |
| **Tracking totals** | `COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE applied = 1)` etc. in `admin_tracking_totals()` |
| **Admin UI** | Clearer hints; removed misleading “New jobs” column; show countries store / Redis in config |

All **4 admin API tests** and **27 related tests** pass.

After deploy, hard-refresh the admin page (`admin.js?v=8`). You should see:
- **Overview** — raw catalog totals (companies, all stored roles, fetch issues)
- **Your panel stats** — your actionable open roles, applied, rejections, etc.
- **Last fetch meta** — last fetch time and company count only (no stale “new jobs” column)

If anything still looks off after refresh, tell me which section and number — we can compare it to a direct DB query.

You're right — that's the correct rule. Not-for-me positions should never inflate open-role totals; they belong only in a dedicated not-for-me count.

## What changed

**Open roles** now explicitly exclude not-for-me jobs (in addition to applied and rejected):
- Server: `_actionable_open_job()` in `panel/stats.py` — open, visa, and per-country counts all use it
- Client: `computeViewStats()` in `board-filter.js` — same rule for board header stats

**Not-for-me is tracked separately:**
- Stats path counts hidden/wrong-location/auto-excluded roles per company
- `compute_stats()` returns `positions_not_for_me`
- Admin panel stats dashboard shows a **Not for me** card
- Admin overview shows **Not for me (all users)** from tracking totals

## How to read the numbers

| Stat | Includes not-for-me? |
|------|------------------------|
| **Open roles** | No |
| **Not for me** | Yes (only here) |
| **Rejections** | No (separate bucket) |
| **Catalog roles** (overview) | Yes — raw DB count, all stored jobs regardless of your tracking |

So if you marked 334 roles as not-for-me, they won't appear in **Open roles** — only under **Not for me**.

input.plan:
# Admin panel data cleanup

## Problem (what looks wrong today)

The admin page stacks **three different counting models** without clear labels, so numbers disagree and feel broken:

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  subgraph overview [Overview - raw DB]
    catalogRoles["Catalog roles ~578"]
    catalogCos["Companies ~165"]
    allUsersNfm["Not for me all users"]
  end
  subgraph panelStats [Your panel stats - user overlay]
    openRoles["Open roles ~197"]
    openCos["Companies ~N"]
    userNfm["Not for me yours"]
  end
  subgraph catalogTable [Catalog tables - raw DB again]
    jobsCol["Jobs column"]
    fetchIssuesCol["Fetch issues"]
    metaTable["Last fetch meta"]
    footnote["Stored roles footnote"]
  end
  catalogRoles -.->|"same number"| jobsCol
  catalogRoles -.->|"same number"| footnote
  fetchIssuesCol -.->|"count only"| fetchProblemsTable["Fetch problems table"]
```

| Issue | Example |
|-------|---------|
| **Same label, different meaning** | Overview **Companies** (165 in catalog) vs panel stats **Companies** (companies with open roles) |
| **Misleading “all positions”** | **Catalog roles** (578 stored in Postgres) shown next to **Open roles** (197 actionable for you) |
| **Triple-counted fetch issues** | Overview card + panel stats card + country table + dedicated table |
| **Duplicate tracking totals** | Overview **Applied / Not for me (all users)** vs panel stats **Applied total / Not for me** (yours) — confusing for a single-user setup |
| **Low-value internals** | **Tracking rows**, **Companies applied**, legacy **Archives**, full keyword/ATS dumps |
| **Redundant catalog sub-tables** | **Last fetch meta** repeats company counts; footnote repeats visa/stored totals |
| **Wrong fetch UX on EC2** | Manual **Country fetch** is disabled (`PANEL_SCRAPE_ENABLED=0` on panel); worker runs on schedule instead |

Correctness of panel stats logic was fixed recently; the remaining pain is **presentation and redundancy**.

---

## Target layout (after cleanup)

1. **Fetch worker** — status strip (replaces Country fetch form)
2. **Your pipeline** — one stats block (open / not-for-me / rejected / applied / new today / last fetch / visa)
3. **Catalog by country** — single table (+ optional ATS sidebar)
4. **Fetch problems** — actionable company list (keep)
5. **Recent fetch runs** — collapsed by default, limit 15
6. **Users** — keep slim table (you chose to keep multi-user visibility)
7. **System** — minimal config (no legacy dumps)

**Remove entirely:** Overview section (`#adminOverview`), Last fetch meta sub-table, catalog footnote, verbose config `<details>` blocks.

---

## Implementation

### 1. Replace Country fetch UI with worker status

**Files:** [`relocation_jobs/static/admin.html`](relocation_jobs/static/admin.html), [`relocation_jobs/static/js/admin-scrape.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/admin-scrape.js) (rename/slim), new small module or inline in [`admin.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/admin.js)

- Remove manual fetch controls (country/ATS/concurrency/start) from production-facing admin.
- New section **Fetch worker** showing:
  - Running fetch (from existing `build_fetch_status()` / `get_running_fetch_run()`)
  - Last completed country fetch (most recent finished `fetch_runs` row with `scope=country`)
  - Note: “Scheduled every 6h via `relocation-fetch-worker`” (static copy; optional env passthrough later)
- Keep fetch progress modal **only** if a fetch is actively running (reuse existing backdrop from admin-scrape when `fetch.running`).

**Backend:** Add `worker` block to [`get_admin_dashboard()`](relocation_jobs/admin/service.py):

```python
{
  "fetch": build_fetch_status(),
  "last_country_run": fetch_repo.latest_finished_country_run(),  # new thin query
  "panel_scrape_enabled": scrape_enabled,
}
```

New repo helper in [`relocation_jobs/fetch/repo.py`](relocation_jobs/fetch/repo.py): one SQL row — latest finished country-wide run.

Optionally pass read-only schedule env to panel container in [`scripts/ec2_app_deploy.sh`](scripts/ec2_app_deploy.sh) (`FETCH_SCHEDULE_INTERVAL_HOURS`, `FETCH_SCHEDULE_COUNTRIES`) for display only.

### 2. Remove Overview section; merge stats into one load

**Files:** [`admin.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/admin.js), [`admin/service.py`](relocation_jobs/admin/service.py), [`web/routes/admin.py`](relocation_jobs/web/routes/admin.py)

- Delete `renderOverview()` and `#adminOverview` from HTML.
- Include `panel_stats` in `GET /api/admin/dashboard` response (call `flatten_companies_for_stats` + `compute_stats` server-side once).
- Remove second client call to `/api/admin/panel-stats` from `loadDashboard()` (keep route for tests/back-compat).
- Slim ops header inside **Your pipeline**: user count + scrape/worker one-liner only if needed.

### 3. Trim panel stats cards

**File:** [`stats-dashboard.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/stats-dashboard.js)

Add admin-only mode OR a `buildAdminStatsHtml()` wrapper that shows:

| Keep | Remove (redundant elsewhere) |
|------|------------------------------|
| Open roles | Fetch issues (catalog + problems table) |
| Co. with open roles (renamed label) | Companies applied |
| Not for me | |
| Rejections | |
| Applied total | |
| New today | |
| Last fetch | |
| Visa / relocation | |
| Applied today (+ list) | |

Rename labels to avoid ambiguity:
- **Open roles** (unchanged)
- **Co. with open roles** (was “Companies”)
- Section title: **Your pipeline** (was “Your panel stats”)

### 4. Consolidate catalog section

**File:** [`admin.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/admin.js) `renderCatalog()`

- Rename column **Jobs** → **Stored roles** (raw Postgres, not your open board).
- Add **Last fetch** column to the main country table (from `country_meta.last_fetch` already in API).
- Remove **Last fetch meta** sub-table.
- Remove footnote (`Empty companies · Visa roles · Stored roles`).
- Keep **ATS breakdown** as a compact side panel (not duplicated elsewhere).
- Drop **Fetch issues** column from country table if the dedicated **Fetch problems** table is present (count is less useful than the list). Alternatively keep count only in country table and drop overview-style duplication — not both.

### 5. Slim Users + Fetch runs + Config

**Users** ([`admin.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/admin.js)):
- Remove **Tracking** column (sum of applied+rejected+hidden is implicit).
- Rename **Hidden** → **Not for me**.

**Fetch runs:**
- Default limit **15** in dashboard API.
- Wrap in `<details>` collapsed by default (“Recent fetch runs”).

**Config** ([`admin.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/admin.js) + optionally [`admin/service.py`](relocation_jobs/admin/service.py)):
- Show only: Database, Countries store (Redis), Scrape on this host, Registration, Concurrency.
- Remove from UI: `data_dir`, `archives`, `httpx`, keyword/ATS `<details>` dumps, `known_ats_companies` list (keep count in backend if needed elsewhere).

### 6. Docs + tests

- Update [`docs/reference/stats.md`](docs/reference/stats.md): admin layout, removed overview block.
- Update [`tests/web/test_catalog_api.py`](tests/web/test_catalog_api.py): assert `panel_stats` embedded in dashboard; keep `/api/admin/panel-stats` test.
- Bump cache buster in `admin.html`.

---

## Out of scope (follow-up if still slow)

Panel stats still take ~90–120s via full-catalog flatten. Cleanup fixes **correctness and clutter**, not load time. A later phase could add SQL aggregate counts for admin-only display ([`board-read-model-proposal.md`](docs/reference/board-read-model-proposal.md)).

---

## Verification

- Load `/admin` on kuchup.com after deploy: one loading pass, no duplicate fetch-issue counts, open roles ≠ stored roles with clear labels.
- Worker section shows running state when scheduler is active.
- `pytest tests/web/test_catalog_api.py -o addopts=`

Trim users table, collapse fetch runs, minimal system config

def latest_finished_country_run() -> dict | None:
    row = get_connection().execute(
        """
        SELECT f.*, u.username
        FROM fetch_runs f
        JOIN users u ON u.id = f.user_id
        WHERE f.status != 'running'
          AND f.scope = 'country'
          AND (f.company_name IS NULL OR TRIM(f.company_name) = '')
        ORDER BY f.finished_at DESC, f.id DESC
        LIMIT 1
        """
    ).fetchone()
    if not row:
        return None
    data = _fetch_run_row_to_dict(row)
    data["username"] = row.get("username")
    return data

from __future__ import annotations

import os

from relocation_jobs.core.ats_constants import (
    DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY,
    EXCLUDE_KEYWORDS,
    INCLUDE_KEYWORDS,
    KNOWN_ATS,
    MAX_CONCURRENCY,
)
from relocation_jobs.core.location_tags import SUGGESTED_CITIES, all_country_labels, load_custom_cities, load_custom_countries
from relocation_jobs.core.paths import COUNTRY_ARCHIVE_FILENAMES, country_archive_filename, data_dir, supported_countries
from relocation_jobs.db import list_users_with_stats, user_count
from relocation_jobs.catalog.custom_countries import countries_use_redis
from relocation_jobs.catalog.repo import get_catalog_overview
from relocation_jobs.core.redis_client import ping_redis, redis_enabled
from relocation_jobs.fetch import repo as fetch_repo
from relocation_jobs.panel.service import flatten_companies_for_stats
from relocation_jobs.panel.stats import compute_stats


def get_system_config(*, scrape_enabled: bool, httpx_available: bool) -> dict:
    custom = load_custom_cities()
    archives = [country_archive_filename(key) for key in sorted(supported_countries())]
    return {
        "database": "postgres",
        "redis": "connected" if countries_use_redis() else ("configured" if redis_enabled() else "off"),
        "redis_ping": ping_redis() if redis_enabled() else False,
        "countries_store": "redis" if countries_use_redis() else "postgres",
        "data_dir": str(data_dir()),
        "scrape_enabled": scrape_enabled,
        "allow_register": os.environ.get("PANEL_ALLOW_REGISTER", "").lower()
        in ("1", "true", "yes"),
        "httpx_available": httpx_available,
        "default_concurrency": DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY,
        "max_concurrency": MAX_CONCURRENCY,
        "include_keywords": INCLUDE_KEYWORDS,
        "exclude_keywords": EXCLUDE_KEYWORDS,
        "known_ats_count": len(KNOWN_ATS),
        "known_ats_companies": sorted(KNOWN_ATS.keys()),
        "suggested_cities": {key: len(values) for key, values in SUGGESTED_CITIES.items()},
        "custom_cities": custom,
        "custom_countries": load_custom_countries(),
        "countries": [
            {"id": key, "label": label} for key, label in sorted(all_country_labels().items())
        ],
        "archives": archives,
    }


def _schedule_interval_hours() -> float:
    raw = (os.environ.get("FETCH_SCHEDULE_INTERVAL_HOURS") or "6").strip()
    try:
        return max(0.25, float(raw))
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        return 6.0


def _schedule_concurrency() -> int:
    raw = (os.environ.get("FETCH_SCHEDULE_CONCURRENCY") or "4").strip()
    try:
        return max(1, min(int(raw), MAX_CONCURRENCY))
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        return 4


def get_worker_status(*, fetch_state: dict | None, scrape_enabled: bool) -> dict:
    return {
        "fetch": fetch_state or {"running": False},
        "last_country_run": fetch_repo.latest_finished_country_run(),
        "panel_scrape_enabled": scrape_enabled,
        "schedule_enabled": os.environ.get("FETCH_SCHEDULE_ENABLED", "0").lower()
        not in ("0", "false", "no"),
        "schedule_interval_hours": _schedule_interval_hours(),
        "schedule_concurrency": _schedule_concurrency(),
        "schedule_countries": (os.environ.get("FETCH_SCHEDULE_COUNTRIES") or "").strip(),
    }


def compute_admin_panel_stats(
    *,
    user_id: int,
    timezone_name: str | None = None,
) -> dict:
    companies, file_meta, fetch_problem_count = flatten_companies_for_stats(
        country_key=None,
        user_id=user_id,
    )
    return compute_stats(
        companies,
        file_meta,
        fetch_problem_count=fetch_problem_count,
        user_id=user_id,
        country_key=None,
        timezone_name=timezone_name,
    )


def get_admin_overview(*, fetch_state: dict | None = None) -> dict:
    catalog = get_catalog_overview()
    return {
        "users": user_count(),
        "catalog": catalog["totals"],
        "fetch": fetch_state or {"running": False},
    }


def get_admin_dashboard(
    *,
    fetch_state: dict | None = None,
    scrape_enabled: bool,
    httpx_available: bool,
    fetch_runs_limit: int = 15,
    user_id: int | None = None,
    timezone_name: str | None = None,
) -> dict:

# Panel statistics

**Last updated:** 2026-07-08

How admin/user stats are computed. Implementation: `panel/stats.py`, `static/js/stats-dashboard.js`.

> Stats are not yet a dedicated domain module — they are derived at read time from flattened catalog + tracking + `fetch_runs`. A future `stats/` package should own these queries.

---

## Admin dashboard (`GET /api/admin/dashboard`)

Single load for the admin page. Includes:

| Block | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| **worker** | Fetch scheduler status, current run, last country fetch |
| **panel_stats** | Your actionable pipeline (same fields as below) |
| **catalog** | Raw Postgres catalog by country |
| **users** | Per-user tracking summary |
| **runs** | Recent fetch runs (default 15) |
| **config** | Minimal system settings |

Legacy route `GET /api/admin/panel-stats` remains for tests; prefer embedded `panel_stats`.

### Your pipeline (`panel_stats`)

Per-user totals over the **full catalog** (not the current board page). Uses `flatten_companies_for_stats()` + `compute_stats()`.

| Stat | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **Open roles** | Main-board jobs you can still act on: in the `jobs` bucket, **not applied**, **not not-for-me**. Excludes rejected roles. |
| **Co. with open roles** | Companies with at least one open role. |
| **New today** | Sum of `fetch_runs.new_jobs` for your account finished **today** (browser timezone). True new discoveries only — not jobs re-enriched on an existing fetch. |
| **Not for me** | Roles in the `not_for_me_jobs` bucket (user-hidden, wrong location, expired, etc.). Never included in open roles. |

**Job dates:** `fetched` = first time the job entered the catalog; `last_seen` = last ATS scrape. Post-fetch visa enrich must **never** overwrite `fetched` (see `scrape/enrich.py`). If many cards show today's date incorrectly, run `python scripts/repair_job_fetched_dates.py --dry-run` then without `--dry-run`.
| **Last fetch** | Latest company activity timestamp in scope. |
| **Applied today / total** | From `job_tracking` + status history. |
| **Rejections** | Jobs in the `rejected_jobs` bucket. |
| **Visa / relocation** | Open roles with `visa_sponsorship=true`. |

**Catalog table:** **Stored roles** = raw `matching_jobs` count (all users, no tracking overlay). Use **Your pipeline** for actionable numbers.

---

## Board header chip

`GET /api/board` returns lightweight `user_stats`; `latest_fetch_new_jobs` uses the same **New today** logic via `fetch_runs`.

---

## Legacy field

`country_meta.last_fetch_new_jobs` is updated only on country-wide fetch completion. **Do not use** for UI stats — kept for catalog metadata only.

from pathlib import Path

from relocation_jobs.core.paths import STATIC_DIR


def test_admin_page_serves_cleanup_layout(v2_auth_client):

    assert 'id="adminOverview"' not in html
    assert "admin-scrape.js" not in html


def test_admin_js_loads_dashboard_once(v2_auth_client):
    del v2_auth_client
    admin_js = (Path(STATIC_DIR) / "js" / "admin.js").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert admin_js.count("/api/admin/dashboard") >= 1
    assert "/api/admin/panel-stats" not in admin_js
    assert "renderOverview" not in admin_js
    assert "adminOverview" not in admin_js


def test_admin_dashboard_worker_last_country_run(v2_auth_client, seeded_catalog_v2, db):
    from relocation_jobs.db import get_user_by_username
    from relocation_jobs.fetch import repo as fetch_repo

    del seeded_catalog_v2, db
    user_id = get_user_by_username("admin")["id"]
    run_id = int(fetch_repo.create_fetch_run(
        user_id=user_id,
        country="uk",
        company_name=None,
        file_name="uk.json",
        concurrency=2,
        started_at="2025-07-01T10:00:00+00:00",
    )["id"])
    fetch_repo.finalize_fetch_run(
        run_id,
        finished_at="2025-07-01T10:30:00+00:00",
        exit_code=0,
        new_jobs=7,
        companies_done=3,
        companies_total=3,
        result_line="Done",
    )
    company_run_id = int(fetch_repo.create_fetch_run(
        user_id=user_id,
        country="uk",
        company_name="Acme Backend Ltd",
        file_name="uk.json",
        concurrency=1,
        started_at="2025-07-02T11:00:00+00:00",
    )["id"])
    fetch_repo.finalize_fetch_run(
        company_run_id,
        finished_at="2025-07-02T11:05:00+00:00",
        exit_code=0,
        new_jobs=99,
    )

    payload = v2_auth_client.get("/api/admin/dashboard?limit=15&timezone=UTC").get_json()
    worker = payload["worker"]
    assert worker["fetch"]["running"] is False
    assert worker["panel_scrape_enabled"] is False
    last = worker["last_country_run"]
    assert last is not None
    assert last["scope"] == "country"
    assert last["country"] == "uk"
    assert last["new_jobs"] == 7
    assert last["company_name"] in (None, "")


def test_admin_dashboard_panel_stats_matches_standalone(v2_auth_client, seeded_catalog_v2):

    standalone = v2_auth_client.get("/api/admin/panel-stats?timezone=UTC").get_json()
    embedded = dashboard["panel_stats"]
    assert embedded is not None
    for key in (
        "total_jobs",
        "companies_with_jobs",
        "positions_applied",
        "positions_not_for_me",
        "positions_rejected",
        "latest_fetch_new_jobs",
        "visa_sponsored",
    ):
        assert embedded[key] == standalone[key]
